location.office_time_required: preference for in-person; no hard requirement stated

level_signals: 3+ years in enablement, sales, or SDR
First dedicated hire for this function — foundational, high-ownership role
Partners with ClayDR leadership and managers but does not manage people directly

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 3+ years in an enablement, sales, or SDR, ideally in a high-growth SaaS environment.
must_haves.qualifier: ideally in a high-growth SaaS environment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have either carried an SDR/outbound number yourself or directly coached SDRs/BDRs responsible for pipeline generation.
You deeply understand the outbound motion and what separates strong pipeline from weak pipeline.
You're a strong communicator and facilitator who's comfortable running workshops, certifications, and executive-level conversations.
You've built and scaled enablement programs including: onboarding, ever-boarding, certifications, and playbooks that improve performance.
You have experience building content and managing enablement systems (e.g., Notion, Gong, or an LMS) to centralize resources and measure adoption.
You thrive in fast-paced, high-growth environments and know how to bring structure and clarity without slowing teams down.
You're curious, collaborative, and excited to partner across GTM to help teams drive growth.

scope.surface: ClayDR (SDR) organization enablement — onboarding, training, playbooks, certifications, and performance programs
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, GTM / outbound sales motion
core_responsibilities: Design and scale onboarding and ever-boarding programs that accelerate ramp time and set a consistent foundation for success.
Build and run workshops and certifications that strengthen ClayDR skills across cold calling, outbound messaging, prospecting, qualification, objection handling, and follow-up.
Partner with ClayDR leadership to identify performance gaps across activity, conversion, messaging, and pipeline generation, then build enablement programs that address them.
Capture and scale best practices from top-performers through repeatable frameworks and playbooks.
Develop and maintain a central hub of resources (playbooks, talk tracks, case studies, templates) to reduce friction and improve knowledge sharing.
Translate new product releases and workflows into clear, outbound plays that drive adoption, retention, and expansion.
Partner with ClayDR managers to reinforce coaching, inspect adoption, and ensure enablement translates into consistent team behaviors.
Measure enablement impact through adoption, performance, engagement, and revenue outcomes.

compensation.equity_form: community equity round mentioned for customers, agency partners, and club members; employee equity implied via tender offers at $5B valuation

green_flags: First dedicated hire in the role — high ownership and visibility
Company recently raised $100M Series C and crossed $100M ARR — strong growth trajectory
Equity participation through tender offers at $5B valuation signals meaningful upside
Global Enablement team structure suggests organizational support for the function
Flexible vacation policy with at least 2 weeks required fully-disconnected
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed anywhere in the JD
JD says 'Remote work. Though we prefer people being in-person' — ambiguity around true flexibility; office is Manhattan-based
First hire in the role means building from scratch with potentially undefined processes and tooling
No explicit LMS named; tooling maturity for enablement function is unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, scope of first-hire role, and cross-functional partnership expectations)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and GTM context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as KPIs)
compensation.equity_form (inferred from company equity context; not explicitly tied to this role's package)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'Remote work. Though we prefer people being in-person' language)
ambiguities: Location type is ambiguous: JD lists a Manhattan office as the base and states 'we prefer people being in-person', but also says 'Remote work' is provided as a benefit. Treated as hybrid with remote flexibility.

The 'ideally in a high-growth SaaS environment' qualifier on the 3+ years requirement is soft language; the core years requirement is treated as hard while the SaaS context is noted as a qualifier rather than a separate hard requirement.
No compensation range is stated anywhere in the JD despite 'Competitive salary' being mentioned.
